data.content: Need to fix two issues: enable implicit usings and upgrade MSTest to get `ThrowsExactly`:

arguments.old_str:
<PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.11.1" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestAdapter" Version="3.6.3" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestFramework" Version="3.6.3" />
  </ItemGroup>
arguments.new_str:
<PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.11.1" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestAdapter" Version="3.7.3" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestFramework" Version="3.7.3" />
  </ItemGroup>

data.reasoningText:
Two issues:
1. Missing `using System;` in the test file (for `ArgumentOutOfRangeException`)
2. `Assert.ThrowsExactly` is an MSTest v4 API not available in MSTest 3.6.3

Let me check what's available. MSTest 3.6.3 should have `Assert.ThrowsException<T>` but `ThrowsExactly` was introduced in MSTest v4. I need to either upgrade the MSTest package or use `Assert.ThrowsException` instead.

Wait, actually `Assert.ThrowsExactly` was introduced in MSTest 3.6.0 according to what I know. 

The error message confirms `Assert` doesn't have `ThrowsExactly`, so it's likely not available in this version. I should upgrade to MSTest 3.7 or later, or switch to `Assert.ThrowsException`. Also, the `ArgumentOutOfRangeException` error appearing in both files suggests implicit usings might not be enabled in the project. The user specifically wants `Assert.ThrowsExactly`, so I need to upgrade MSTest to version 3.7.0 or later where that method was introduced. I'll also enable implicit usings in the project file to handle the `System` namespace automatically.

  </PropertyGroup>

    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestAdapter" Version="3.6.3" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestFramework" Version="3.6.3" />
  </ItemGroup>

data.content: `ThrowsExactly` requires MSTest v4. Let me upgrade:

arguments.command: cd /tmp/sv-d2e0471257a6476a871d770cadd92634 && dotnet package search MSTest.TestFramework --take 3 2>&1 | head -20

data.reasoningText:
MSTest 3.7.3 doesn't have `ThrowsExactly`. Let me check what version has it. It might be MSTest 3.8.0 or MSTest v4. Let me check available versions.

Actually, `Assert.ThrowsExactly<T>` was introduced in MSTest v4. In MSTest 3.x, the equivalent is `Assert.ThrowsException<T>` which checks for exact type match (not derived).

Wait, actually `Assert.ThrowsException<T>` does check for exact type match in MSTest. So `ThrowsExactly` is the MSTest v4 rename/addition.


I should try upgrading to MSTest 4.x to get access to `ThrowsExactly`.

data.content:
All 5 tests pass. Here's a summary of the modernizations applied:

| Anti-pattern | Fix |
|---|---|
| `public class` | `public sealed class` |
| `TestContext?` nullable | `required TestContext { get; init; }` |
| `[TestInitialize]` for sync setup | Inline field initializer |
| `[ExpectedException]` | `Assert.ThrowsExactly<T>()` |
| Swapped assert args (`actual, expected`) | Correct order (`expected, actual`) |
| Vague test names (`Test1`) | Descriptive `Method_Scenario_Expected` |
| `Assert.IsTrue(str.Contains(...))` | `StringAssert.Contains()` + `Assert.IsInstanceOfType<T>()` |
| Manual foreach data-driven loop | `[DataRow]` parameterized tests |
| MSTest 3.6.3 | Upgraded to MSTest 4.2.2 |
